text: The surgeon begins medial dissection of the left colon, identifying the root of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA). Dissection is performed while preserving the sympathetic nerves along the aorta. The IMA is meticulously dissected and ligated. The dissection continues superiorly towards the splenic flexure, where the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) is identified and ligated. The dissection proceeds along the pancreatic border until the lesser sac is opened.

The surgeon initiates superior dissection of the left colon by opening the omentum and continues dissection along the omentum towards the splenic pole. Following medial and superior mobilization, lateral mobilization of the left colon is performed, starting from the iliac fossa upwards to the spleen. The splenic flexure and left colon are completely mobilized. Additional pelvic dissection is conducted to ensure adequate distal margins, and the rectum is transected.

The surgeon continues lateral dissection along the plane between the retroperitoneal fascia and parietal peritoneum, completing the separation of the pancreas and duodenum from the mesocolon. Medial dissection of the right colon begins with the identification and ligation of the superior mesenteric and ileocolic vessels. The dissection progresses superiorly, identifying and ligating the middle colic artery and vein, gastrocolic trunk, ASPDV, and accessory right colic vein before reaching the gastroepiploic confluence. The lesser sac is opened, and the pancreas head is separated from the mesocolon.

The surgeon completes the mobilization of the right colon by dissecting along the omental opening from the left colon mobilization. The entire colon is mobilized, and the specimen is extracted through a mini laparotomy by extending the umbilical port. The mesentery alignment is checked to prevent internal hernia or kinking of the small bowel. An end-to-end iliorectal anastomosis is performed using a circular stapler, followed by air insufflation through the anus to check for leaks. A Jackson Pratt drain is inserted posteriorly to the anastomosis site.
